meta.title: Platform
meta.description: How the Viziblezone situational awareness platform works: direct device-to-device presence broadcasting, on-device risk analysis, and integration paths for OEMs, cities and industry.

hero.title:
Awareness without
infrastructure
hero.lede: Viziblezone is a software layer that runs on the devices people already carry. No towers, no cameras, no rollout projects — presence and risk flow directly between phones, vehicles and systems.

how.title:
Three moves,
seconds earlier
how.lede: The platform closes the perception gap that every line-of-sight sensor shares, by letting endangered and endangering parties announce themselves to each other directly.

steps.title: Announce presence
steps.text: A protected phone continuously broadcasts a compact signature: presence, motion and trajectory. Fully anonymous, battery-friendly, no app interaction needed.

steps.title: Device to device
steps.text: Nearby vehicles, phones and systems receive the signal directly — no cell tower, no cloud, no network in between. Signals travel through and around obstructions.

steps.title: Alert before sight
steps.text: An on-device risk engine fuses trajectories and issues a targeted alert seconds before line of sight — enough time to brake, slow down or reroute.

principles.title:
What the platform
refuses to depend on

items.title: Anonymous by design
items.text: Broadcast signatures carry motion and risk context, not identity. No accounts, no tracking, no personal data leaving the device.

items.title: No single point of failure
items.text: Every interaction is peer to peer. There is no server whose outage silences the system and no coverage map that limits where it works.

items.title: Beyond line of sight
items.text: Signals propagate where optics cannot — through and around vehicles, structures and terrain — giving seconds of warning that no camera can.

items.title: Devices people already carry
items.text: Deployment is a software update, not a construction project. Coverage grows with adoption, starting from day one.

integration.title: Three ways in
integration.lede: The platform is built to meet partners where they are — from a pilot in a defined zone to deep OEM integration.

paths.title: Device & OS partners
paths.text: Native integration with smartphone platforms and OEM software stacks, bringing protection to entire device fleets at once.

paths.title: Vehicle & ADAS
paths.text: Alerts delivered into infotainment, ADAS and fleet systems, extending the vehicle's perception envelope beyond its own sensors.

paths.title: Site & city pilots
paths.text: A scoped deployment in a port, campus, work site or urban zone — measurable safety outcomes within a defined perimeter.

items.q: Does it need cellular coverage or internet?
items.a: No. Communication is direct, device to device. The platform is designed to keep working in dead zones, underground facilities, and full network outages — that is a core design requirement, not an edge case.
No. The broadcast is anonymous and carries no identity. It announces that someone is there and how they are moving — not who they are. Nothing is uploaded to a cloud.
The broadcast is engineered for continuous background operation with negligible battery impact, using the low-power capabilities of modern smartphone hardware.

items.q: How is this different from cameras, radar or V2X infrastructure?
items.a: Those approaches either require line of sight or require infrastructure to be built and maintained. Viziblezone requires neither: it sees around obstacles and rides on devices that are already in everyone's pocket.
For a pilot: an app or SDK on participating devices in a defined zone. For partners: integration into the device or vehicle software stack. There is no hardware to install in the field.

cta.title:
See it working
live
cta.lede: The fastest way to understand the platform is to watch a hidden pedestrian appear on screen before you can see them.
